paper_metadata.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
paper_metadata.abstract: Transfer learning, where a model is first pre-trained on a data-rich task before being fine-tuned on a downstream task, has emerged as a powerful technique in natural language processing (NLP). The effectiveness of transfer learning has given rise to a diversity of approaches, methodology, and practice. In this paper, we explore the landscape of transfer learning techniques for NLP by introducing a unified framework that converts all text-based language problems into a text-to-text format. Our systematic study compares pre-training objectives, architectures, unlabeled data sets, transfer approaches, and other factors on dozens of language understanding tasks. By combining the insights from our exploration with scale and our new ``Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus'', we achieve state-of-the-art results on many benchmarks covering summarization, question answering, text classification, and more. To facilitate future work on transfer learning for NLP, we release our data set, pre-trained models, and code.

extraction_data.innovation: T5 introduces a unified text-to-text framework for transfer learning, where all tasks are reformulated as text-to-text problems, enabling a single model to handle diverse NLP tasks through pre-training and fine-tuning.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on multiple NLP tasks (translation, summarization, question answering)
extraction_data.research_problem: Unified transfer learning across diverse NLP tasks
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, machine translation, text summarization, question answering
extraction_data.paper_title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
